sources.name: List of chief ministers of West Bengal
sources.authority: Wikipedia (CC BY-SA 4.0; verified against ECI Statistical Reports per term)

sources.name: West Bengal Legislative Assembly Election 2026 partywise result

terms.notes: Third term as CM in the continuous Left Front rule that began in 1977. Left Front (CPI(M)-led, with CPI, RSP, AIFB and others) won the 1987 election.
Fourth term. Left Front won the 1991 election.
Fifth and final term. Left Front won the 1996 election. Basu stepped down on 5 November 2000 citing age and health, handing over to Buddhadeb Bhattacharjee mid-term without a fresh election.

references.note: Voluntary retirement, handed over to Bhattacharjee.

terms.notes: First stint, completing Basu's fifth term. Continued into a fresh term after the 2001 election.

references.note: Two-term CM in his own right (2001-06, 2006-11) plus the partial 2000-01 hand-over.

terms.notes: Full first elected term. Left Front won the 2001 election.
Second elected term. Left Front won the 2006 election by a large margin (235/294 seats) but the term saw the Singur and Nandigram crises that ultimately cost the LF its three-decade hold on power.
First term and end of 34 years of continuous Left Front rule. All India Trinamool Congress (AITC) won the 2011 election in pre-poll alliance with INC, securing 184/294 seats (INC 42).
Second term. AITC contested 2016 solo and won 211/294 seats. No formal pre-poll alliance.

Third term. AITC won the 2021 election with 213/294 seats against a high-spend BJP challenge (BJP secured 77). AITC joined the INDIA bloc at its formation in July 2023 but remained an independent regional pole at the state level.
Placeholder for the post-May-2026 government. Results ingested in datasets/elections/AcGenMay2026/S25/. Backfill once the new ministry is sworn in.
